seoTitle: How to Download an Excel Sheet From the Cloud
metaDescription: Save a cloud-based Excel spreadsheet to your computer from Microsoft 365 Web, Google Sheets, or an email attachment while keeping formulas intact.
intro: Whether your spreadsheet lives in Microsoft 365 Web, Google Sheets, or an inbox attachment, getting a clean local copy shouldn't be complicated. This guide covers the download path for each source and how to confirm your formulas and formatting survive the trip.

steps.heading: Start from Microsoft 365 Web
steps.body: Open your browser workbook and click the File tab in the top left corner.

steps.heading: Select Save As
steps.body: Navigate the vertical menu and click Save As to reveal export options.

steps.heading: Download a copy
steps.body: Click Download a Copy, then confirm the prompt to trigger the local export.

steps.heading: Convert a Google Sheet
steps.body: In Google Sheets, go to File, hover over Download, and select Microsoft Excel (.xlsx).

steps.heading: Save from an email attachment
steps.body: Hover over the attached file in your message and click the Download arrow.

steps.heading: Choose your save location
steps.body: Select a destination folder in your file explorer and confirm your saved file opens correctly.

faq.question: Does downloading from the cloud preserve my formulas?
faq.answer: Yes, the export process is built to preserve active formulas, number formatting, and grid metadata in the local .xlsx file.

faq.question: Is there a difference between exporting from Microsoft 365 Web versus Google Sheets?
faq.answer: The menu steps differ, but both convert your cloud-based file into a native .xlsx you can open locally.

faq.question: What if the file I want is only sent as an email attachment?
faq.answer: Hover over the attachment and click the download arrow, then choose a destination folder to save it locally.

faq.question: Should I check the file after downloading?
faq.answer: Yes, opening the saved file to confirm formulas, fonts, and data ranges display correctly is a good habit before relying on it.
